sections.title: 1. Purpose
sections.section_prompt: State that this PMS Plan describes the systematic collection and analysis of real-world data for {device_name_model} per EU MDR Article 84 and ISO 13485:2016 §8.2.1, feeding risk file {ref:RD-007}, CAPA {ref:CA-001}, vigilance {ref:CA-005}, and PSUR/PMCF {ref:RA-006}.

sections.title: 2. PMS Objectives
clauses: 2.1 Confirm benefit-risk remains acceptable in real-world use
2.2 Detect emerging risks and previously unknown side effects
2.3 Identify trends in complaints, malfunctions, and use errors
2.4 Verify continued conformity with GSPR and applicable standards
2.5 Identify need for preventive, corrective or FSCA actions
sections.section_prompt: For each objective, briefly describe the method used for {device_name_model}.

sections.title: 3. Data Sources & Collection Methods
sections.ai_rows_prompt: Generate 8–12 sources: complaints ({ref:CA-004}), service/repair records, returns analysis, user surveys, sales/usage data, literature review, regulatory database scan (FDA MAUDE, Eudamed, MHRA), social media, similar-device data, PMCF studies, internal audit findings. Return JSON array.

sections.title: 4. Analysis Methods & Thresholds
sections.section_prompt: Describe statistical and trend-analysis methods, signal-detection thresholds (e.g. complaint rate > X per 1000 units triggers investigation), and escalation paths to CAPA ({ref:CA-001}) and risk re-evaluation ({ref:RD-007}).

sections.title: 5. Reporting Cadence
sections.ai_rows_prompt: Include: PMS Trend Report (internal monthly), PSUR (annual for class IIb/III per EU MDR Article 86 — {ref:RA-006}), PMCF Report, Management Review input ({ref:QP-005}), Vigilance reports as triggered ({ref:CA-005}). Return JSON array.

output_config.footer: CONFIDENTIAL — Controlled Document | EU MDR Art. 84
